_meta.description: Token parity findings for mui. Cluster 6 dimensions 6.1 and 6.2. Generated by diff-tokens.mjs.

findings.description: Token value mismatch: "palette/warning/main" has value "#ed6c02" in code but resolves to "#ef6c00" in Figma.
findings.recommendation: Colour value drift between code and Figma. Code has #ed6c02, Figma resolves to #ef6c00 via alias chain orange/800. Determine the authoritative source and correct the other.

findings.description: Token value mismatch: "material/colors/lightGreen/400" has value "#9ccc65" in code but resolves to "#8bc34a" in Figma.
findings.recommendation: Align the token value between code and Figma. Determine which source is authoritative and update the other.

findings.description: Token value mismatch: "typography/h4/fontSize" has value "2.125rem" in code but resolves to "32" in Figma.
findings.recommendation: Align the token value between code and Figma. Determine which source is authoritative and update the other.

findings.description: Token value mismatch: "typography/fontFamily" has value ""Roboto", "Helvetica", "Arial", sans-serif" in code but resolves to "Roboto" in Figma.
findings.recommendation: Figma stores only the primary font family; code includes the full fallback stack. This is a structural scope difference, not a bug. Document the fallback stack in the Figma variable description for parity.

findings.description: Token value mismatch: "breakpoints/xs" has value "0" in code but resolves to "444" in Figma.
findings.recommendation: Code defines xs as the minimum bound (0px); Figma defines it as a design breakpoint (444px). These are different semantic uses of the same name. Align on the meaning or rename the Figma variable to avoid confusion.
